title: scan_state table: CHECK (id = 1) + seeded INSERT OR IGNORE — correct single-row enforcement
description: Why this choice is correct: the scan_state table must have exactly one row so scan_tip() can use fetch_one (fail loudly if missing) rather than fetch_optional (silently return None on first open). The CHECK constraint prevents accidental duplicate inserts at the DB level; INSERT OR IGNORE seeds the row during migration. A contributor might suggest removing the CHECK and using UPSERT on every set_scan_tip call — that works but removes the invariant guarantee and makes scan_tip() fragile. Keep CHECK (id=1) and the seeded row.
close_reason: Decision record — no code change required; rationale preserved in codebase
